sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The RIGHT had no impact - the board didn't change. This means the green object can't move right. Let me check the current positions again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Green/White:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'W'):
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")

print("\nBoxes:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'G') and n['pixels'] == 12:
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check what's blocking RIGHT movement
print("\nAll nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] not in ('w', 'P'):
        print(f"  {n['color']}: boundary={n['boundary']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see the issue. The green N is at rows 24-26, cols 40-43. The box is at rows 24-27, cols 44-47. But the RIGHT had no impact. 
Wait, I notice the white W is at rows 27-31, cols 40-43. The green is at rows 24-26, cols 40-43. So the green and white are stacked vertically.
Looking at the box at [24,44]-[27,47], it has a blue child at [25,45]-[26,46]. The green N at rows 24-26 overlaps with the box rows 24-27 but not columns.
The issue is that the green is 3 rows tall (24-26) and 4 cols wide (40-43). The box is 4 rows tall (24-27) and 4 cols wide (44-47). So the green's right edge is at col 43 and the box's left edge is at col 44. They're adjacent but not overlapping.
But RIGHT had no impact... Maybe there's a wall or the green can't move right because it would collide with the box?
Wait, let me look at the white object. It's at rows 27-31, cols 40-43. So the combined green+white object spans rows 24-31, cols 40-43. The green is on top, white is below.
When I try to move RIGHT, maybe the white part is blocked by something? Let me check if there's anything at rows 27-31, cols 44+.
Actually, I think the issue might be that the green and white move together as one unit. The white is at rows 27-31, and maybe there's something blocking at those rows.
Let me try moving UP first to get the green higher, then RIGHT.
Actually wait - looking more carefully, the green N is at rows 24-26 and the box is at rows 24-27. They overlap in rows 24-26. If I move RIGHT, the green would overlap with the box. But the RIGHT had no impact.
Hmm, maybe the green can't move through the box. Let me try moving UP first to get above the box, then RIGHT, then DOWN to overlap.
Actually, the green is already at row 24, same as the box's top. Let me try UP to see if I can move up.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green N: rows 24-26, cols 40-43
# White W: rows 27-31, cols 40-43
# Box: rows 24-27, cols 44-47
# RIGHT had no impact - maybe blocked by box?
# Let me try UP then RIGHT to go around

action(['UP', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
